subject: Re: filter_quality removing all tiltseries upon use of --ntilts
body:
Thank you Alister! 
On Tuesday, December 17, 2024 at 12:31:23 PM UTC-5 alist...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hey Daniel,
>
> I just took a look and it does seem like this functionality is broken - 
> sorry about that! Have opened an issue and will get to it at some point but 
> no guarantees about when. https://github.com/warpem/warp/issues/320
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> On Sun, 15 Dec 2024 at 00:15, Daniel Cai <dxh...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
>> Hi everyone,
>>
>> I'm trying to use warptools to select and deselect frames and tilts based 
>> on the outputted quality metrics.
>>
>> For the frames, I was able to get a list of good frames and select them 
>> through change_selection. I wanted to confirm with those more knowledgeable 
>> if, by selecting only the frames that pass the filters, only those tilts 
>> would be included into the tilt series stack.
>>
>> The main problem I am facing is when I use
>> WarpTools filter_quality --settings warp_tiltseries.settings --o 
>> quality_tilts.txt --ntilts "N"
>> the filter excludes all tilt series regardless of what the N value is.
>> For example: I have 8/48 tilt series with <30 tilts in the stack. I use 
>> --ntilts 8. It removes all 48. --ntilts 0 and --ntilts 48 also remove all 
>> 48 tilt series. 
>>
>> Am I using --ntilts incorrectly? Or is something else going on?
>>
>> Daniel
>>
>> -- 
>> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups 
>> "Warp" group.
>> To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send an 
>> email to warp-em+u...@googlegroups.com.

>> <https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/e0c338ee-f80e-46f7-8ed0-1fa60be07716n%40googlegroups.com?utm_medium=email&utm_source=footer>
>> .
>>
>
